scope: Required at launch and immutable for the process. Requests cannot override it.

idempotency.revision_precondition: old_confidence_ppm is validated on first execution but excluded from retry identity, allowing the same semantic revision intent to replay after its first success.
idempotency.revision_audit_identity: Each item_revised audit event exposes details.idempotency_key together with details.revision_id so an authenticated client can identify and replay the exact persisted operation.

GET /v1/health.managed_owner: Managed launches expose instance_id, parent_pid, and server_pid. External launches omit it.

POST /v1/control/shutdown.availability: Managed launches only. Bearer authentication, fixed scope, and exact owner instance must all match.

POST /v1/admin/reset.availability: Only with --allow-test-reset. Never enabled by installers.

launch: ATLAS_COGNITIVE_TOKEN=... python integrations/cognitive-service/server.py --db PATH --scope PROFILE --port 8741. Managed clients also pass --owner-instance and --parent-pid. Healthy exact-scope clients attach without ownership; after owner shutdown or parent loss, an attached client may launch and own the replacement.
restart_proof: Stop and relaunch the same command with the same db, scope, and token; item, revision, and proposal IDs must remain identical.
managed_failover: A healthy exact-scope client attaches without ownership. On transport unavailability after health verification, managed clients may fail over and retry the same replay-safe request once.
convergent_path_semantics: Traversal selects one deterministic affected upstream path per convergent node; proposal deltas from multiple converging supports are not aggregated.
cognitive_owner: One Python implementation. SQLite is persistence only and contains no cognitive branching, confidence formula, traversal, contradiction, routing, or idempotency decisions.
